---
title: "Day 2: Pisa to Florence"
subtitle: "Arrival in Italy"
date: "2026-06-05"
day-of-week: "Thursday"
location: "Pisa → Florence"
categories: [itinerary, pisa, florence, arrival]
---

## Overview

**First day in Italy!** Arrive in Pisa from overnight flight, quick exploration of the city, then afternoon train to Florence. This is a gentle landing - managing jet lag while getting our first taste of Italy.

**Today's Goals:**
- Clear customs and immigration
- Brief Pisa sightseeing (Leaning Tower)
- Train to Florence
- Check into hotel
- Stay awake until evening (fight jet lag)
- First Italian dinner

---

## Morning: Arrival in Pisa

### Airport Arrival (~8-11 AM)

**After Clearing Customs:**
1. Look for **ATM** in arrivals hall (withdraw €200-300 for immediate cash needs)
2. Look for **PisaMover** signs (shuttle to train station)
3. Or: If overnighting in Pisa, take taxi to hotel first

**Currency Exchange:**
- Skip airport exchange kiosks (terrible rates)
- Use ATM for best rates
- Credit cards work almost everywhere

### To Pisa Centrale Station

**PisaMover Shuttle:**
- Automated people mover (like airport tram)
- Duration: 8 minutes
- Frequency: Continuous
- From airport directly to Pisa Centrale train station
- Free or minimal cost

**At Pisa Centrale:**
- Luggage storage available if needed (€6-12/bag)
- Can leave bags and explore Pisa lighter

---

## Mid-Morning: Quick Pisa Tour (Optional)

**Time Available:** 2-3 hours depending on flight arrival

### Piazza dei Miracoli

**From Station:** 20-minute walk or bus #LAM Rossa

**The Complex:**
- **Leaning Tower** - The icon (exterior views free, climb requires ticket/booking)
- **Cathedral (Duomo)** - Stunning Romanesque architecture
- **Baptistery** - Incredible acoustics
- **Camposanto** - Monumental cemetery
- **Opera Museum** - Skip unless time permits

**Recommendations:**
- **Don't climb tower** (takes time, requires advance booking)
- **Do take photos** from Campo dei Miracoli lawn (mandatory tourist shot!)
- **Do walk around** the complex (beautiful from all angles)
- **Do visit Cathedral** if open (free entry, stunning interior)
- **Time needed:** 1-1.5 hours for photos and cathedral

### Beyond the Tower

**If Extra Time:**
- **Arno River** - Lovely walk along riverbanks
- **Piazza dei Cavalieri** - Historic square, quieter than Leaning Tower area
- **Local cafés** - Cappuccino and cornetto (Italian breakfast)

**Recommendation:** Keep it light today - you're tired, Florence awaits.

---

## Midday: Train to Florence

### Returning to Pisa Centrale

**If You Stored Luggage:** Retrieve bags from station storage

**If Luggage at Hotel:** Check out, taxi to station

### Pisa → Firenze Train

**See:** [Trains Research](../research/trains.qmd#route-1-pisa-florence) for details

**Train Details:**
- **Type:** Regionale (regional train, no reservation needed)
- **Frequency:** Every 30 minutes
- **Duration:** 50-70 minutes
- **Price:** €10-12 one-way
- **Buy ticket:** At station window or automated machines

**Boarding:**
- Check departure board (Partenze) for platform (binario)
- **Validate ticket** before boarding (yellow machines on platform)
- Board any regional train to "Firenze S.M.N." (Santa Maria Novella)
- No assigned seats (first-come seating)
- Store luggage in racks near doors

**On the Train:**
- Sit back and enjoy your first Italian train ride!
- Tuscan countryside views
- Short journey - don't stress

---

## Afternoon: Arrive Florence

### Firenze Santa Maria Novella (SMN) Station

**Arrival Time:** Varies, but likely 2-4 PM

**At Station:**
- Busy, central station
- Announcements in Italian and English
- Follow "Uscita" (Exit) signs
- Emerge into Piazza della Stazione

**Orientation:**
- Duomo is visible ~10-minute walk northeast
- Most hotels walkable from station
- Taxis available if needed

### To Hotel

**Walking (Recommended):**
- Use Google Maps offline
- Florence is compact and walkable
- Cobblestones - roll luggage carefully
- 5-15 minutes depending on hotel location

**Taxi (If Tired):**
- White taxis at station taxi stand
- ~€10-15 to most central hotels
- Tell driver hotel name and address

### Hotel Check-In

**Typical Check-In:** 2-3 PM (may vary)

**If Early:**
- Most hotels store luggage before check-in
- Can explore while waiting for room

**At Check-In:**
- Passport may be required for registration
- Ask for: WiFi password, breakfast times, area map
- Get recommendations for nearby restaurants

**In Room:**
- Quick shower (you'll feel human again!)
- Change clothes
- **Do NOT nap** - stay awake until evening (fight jet lag)
- Unpack minimally (just what you need for tonight)

---

## Late Afternoon: Gentle Orientation

### First Florence Walk (Light Activity)

**Goal:** Stay awake, gentle exercise, orient yourself

**Suggested Route (30-45 minutes):**
1. Walk toward Duomo (you'll see it from almost anywhere)
2. Piazza del Duomo - marvel at the scale
3. Walk around Cathedral exterior (save climbing for tomorrow)
4. Stroll to Piazza della Signoria
5. Ponte Vecchio (famous medieval bridge)
6. Back to hotel area

**What to Notice:**
- Narrow streets, tall buildings
- Gelato shops everywhere
- Small piazzas
- Tourists mixed with locals
- Italian being spoken around you (you're here!)

**What NOT to Do:**
- Don't enter museums (you're too tired)
- Don't commit to long walks (pace yourself)
- Don't shop seriously (just window browse)

### Aperitivo (6-8 PM)

**The Italian Tradition:**
- Buy a drink (€8-12)
- Free appetizers come with it (small plates, olives, chips, etc.)
- Social hour before dinner

**Where:**
- Any bar with "aperitivo" sign
- Santo Spirito piazza (Oltrarno) very local
- Ask hotel for recommendation

**Order:**
- Aperol Spritz (classic)
- Prosecco
- Negroni (Florentine invention)
- Or just a beer

**Why:** Light food, stays awake, ease into Italian rhythm

---

## Evening: First Italian Dinner

### Dinner (~7:30-9 PM)

**Where to Eat:**

**Option 1: Near Hotel (Easy)**
- Ask hotel concierge for recommendation
- Walk 5-10 minutes
- Authentic trattoria

**Option 2: Across River in Oltrarno**
- More local, less touristy
- Il Santo Bevitore, Osteria dell'Enoteca
- 15-20 minute walk from center

**What to Order:**
- **Don't overthink it** - you're tired
- Simple pasta (cacio e pepe, carbonara, pomodoro)
- Glass of house wine (vino della casa)
- Share a dessert or get gelato after
- Save the big Florentine steak for when you're rested

**Dining Tips:**
- Coperto (cover charge) €1-3 is normal
- Service slower than U.S. (relax, enjoy)
- Ask for "il conto" when ready to pay
- Tipping: Round up or 5-10%, not 20%

### After Dinner

**Gelato Walk:**
- Find nearby gelateria
- Get gelato (stracciatella, pistachio, nocciola are safe bets)
- Stroll back toward hotel
- Enjoy being in FLORENCE

**Evening Reflection:**
- Soak in the fact that you're in Italy
- Tomorrow the real adventure begins
- Early night tonight (jet lag is real)

---

## Bedtime (~9-10 PM)

### Jet Lag Strategy

**DO:**
- Go to bed at reasonable Italian time (9-10 PM)
- Take shower if it helps you sleep
- Set alarm for tomorrow morning
- Charge all devices
- Lay out clothes for tomorrow

**DON'T:**
- Nap during day (pushes bedtime later)
- Stay up super late (tempting but counterproductive)
- Sleep with lights on
- Use phone in bed (blue light prevents sleep)

**Tomorrow:**
- You'll likely wake early (jet lag)
- Use that time! Early morning Florence is magical
- Hit the ground running with museums

---

## Checklist for Today

### Accomplished

- [ ] Cleared immigration/customs in Pisa
- [ ] Got cash from ATM
- [ ] Saw Leaning Tower (quick visit)
- [ ] Train to Florence successful
- [ ] Checked into hotel
- [ ] Stayed awake until evening
- [ ] Had first Italian meal
- [ ] Got gelato
- [ ] In bed by 10 PM

### For Tomorrow (Day 3)

- [ ] Wake early, grab café breakfast
- [ ] Duomo complex visit (book dome climb if not done)
- [ ] Uffizi Gallery (check booking time)
- [ ] Real exploring begins!

---

## Notes & Reflections

### Pisa Impressions

**The Leaning Tower:**

**First thoughts on Italy:**

### Florence Arrival

**First impressions of Florence:**

**Hotel/neighborhood:**

**Dinner experience:**

**Jet lag status:**

**Overall feeling:**

---

## Important Reminders

**Tomorrow (Day 3):**
- **Uffizi Gallery** - Check your timed entry (likely need to book)
- **Duomo Dome Climb** - Must book ahead for timed slot
- Get café breakfast (cappuccino + cornetto = €3-5)
- Wear comfortable shoes (lots of walking)
- Bring water bottle (refill at fountains)
- Camera charged

---

## Links & Resources

- [Trains Research](../research/trains.qmd) - Pisa to Florence details
- [Florence Overview](../regions/florence.qmd) - City context
- [Accommodations](../research/accommodations.qmd) - Hotel info
- [Restaurants](../research/restaurants.qmd) - Dining guide

---

**Previous Day:** [Day 1: Madison to Pisa](day-01-2026-06-04-madison-to-pisa.qmd)

**Next Day:** [Day 3: Florence - Duomo & Uffizi](day-03-2026-06-06-florence.qmd)
